name: ExtensionIngredient_Function
title: R5: Precise action within the drug product, e.g. antioxidant, alkalizing agent (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Ingredient.function` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Ingredient.function` 0..* `CodeableConcept`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Ingredient.function` 0..* `CodeableConcept`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Ingredient.function` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `Ingredient` is not mapped.
Element `Ingredient.function` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Precise action within the drug product, e.g. antioxidant, alkalizing agent (new)
element.comment:
Element `Ingredient.function` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `Ingredient` is not mapped.
Element `Ingredient.function` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: Precise action within the drug product, e.g. antioxidant, alkalizing agent
element.definition: A classification of the ingredient identifying its precise purpose(s) in the drug product. This extends the Ingredient.role to add more detail. Example: antioxidant, alkalizing agent.

binding.description: A classification of the ingredient identifying its precise purpose(s) in the drug product (beyond e.g. active/inactive).
